_description: Domain-level cost data for the assembler. Two numbers per domain: (1) cost_at_zero — annual societal cost per person when a domain scores 0 (complete failure), (2) cost_per_tenth — marginal cost to improve domain score by 10 points. These let the assembler produce per-domain investment estimates: 'moving health from 40 to 60 costs $X and saves $Y'.
_methodology: cost_at_zero derived from published total cost estimates (healthcare for uninsured, homelessness costs, incarceration costs, etc.) divided by affected population. cost_per_tenth is the marginal intervention cost to move from current baseline toward sufficiency, based on program evaluation literature.

_sources.health: Coughlin, T. et al. (2014). Uncompensated Care for the Uninsured. Kaiser Family Foundation.
_sources.housing: Culhane, D. (2008). The Cost of Homelessness. European Journal of Homelessness.
_sources.economics: Holzer, H. et al. (2008). The Economic Costs of Childhood Poverty. NASW.
_sources.education: Levin, H. et al. (2007). The Costs and Benefits of an Excellent Education. Teachers College.
_sources.nutrition: Cook, J. & Jeng, K. (2009). Child Food Insecurity: The Economic Impact. Feeding America.
_sources.safety: Miller, T. et al. (2021). Costs of Fatal and Nonfatal Injury. National Safety Council.

health.zero_scenario: Uninsured, no primary care, ER-dependent, untreated chronic conditions
health.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: insurance enrollment, primary care linkage, chronic disease management, behavioral health integration

housing.zero_scenario: Chronic homelessness: shelter cycling, ER visits, justice involvement
housing.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: rapid rehousing ($5K), rent stabilization, permanent supportive housing ($21K)

economics.zero_scenario: Zero income, no labor force participation, full public assistance dependency
economics.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: workforce development, subsidized employment, EITC expansion, barrier removal

education.zero_scenario: No educational engagement, no credentials, no pathway to skill development
education.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: enrollment support, tutoring, credential programs, Pell access

nutrition.zero_scenario: Severe food insecurity, malnutrition, diet-related chronic disease onset
nutrition.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: SNAP enrollment ($2328/yr), WIC ($564/yr), food bank access, medically tailored meals

safety.zero_scenario: Incarceration or chronic violence exposure, complete loss of personal security
safety.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: violence interruption, reentry programs, community policing, diversion

transportation.zero_scenario: No vehicle, no transit access, isolated from employment and services
transportation.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: transit passes ($1200/yr), ride-to-work programs, vehicle repair assistance

infrastructure.zero_scenario: No internet, no computer, no digital access to services or employment
infrastructure.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: broadband subsidy ($360/yr), device provision, digital literacy training

environment.zero_scenario: Toxic exposure, contaminated water, hazardous housing (lead, mold, asbestos)
environment.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: lead abatement ($10K one-time), air quality remediation, water treatment, Superfund cleanup

legal.zero_scenario: No legal identity, no benefits access, unresolved legal barriers (warrants, records, immigration)
legal.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: legal aid ($1200/yr), record expungement, benefits enrollment assistance, immigration counsel

community.zero_scenario: Complete social isolation, no civic participation, no informal support network
community.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: community center access, peer support programs, civic engagement facilitation

purpose.zero_scenario: No meaningful activity, no goals, no sense of contribution or belonging
purpose.tenth_intervention: Each 10-point improvement: vocational counseling, arts programs, volunteer coordination, mentorship
